site.title: Jun Park
site.description: Independent product designer and developer based in Seoul.

meta.hero_headline: I design quiet, useful interfaces for ambitious teams shaping the next decade of software.
meta.hero_lede: Hi, I'm Jun — an independent product designer and developer based in Seoul. For the last decade I've helped startups and established teams ship calm, considered software. Currently leading design at a small fintech, and taking on a few outside projects each year.
meta.currently_text: Lead designer at Northwind. Writing weekly at Field Notes.
meta.available_text: One advisory engagement opening in Q3 2026.

pages.title: Work
pages.content: Selected projects from the last few years.

pages.title: About
pages.content:
I'm Jun, a product designer and developer based in Seoul. For the last decade I've worked with founders and small teams to ship software that respects the people using it.

My background is in industrial design — I trained in Milan before moving into screens. I think about software the way I was taught to think about objects: scale, weight, material, and the way it sits in someone's hand.

Outside of client work I write a small newsletter on the craft of interface design, and I keep an irregular reading list of essays I find useful.

## Working notes

| Practice | Rhythm |
| --- | --- |
| Product strategy | Weekly critique |
| Interface systems | Design and implementation |
| Writing | Field notes and essays |

- [x] Keep the work quiet and useful.
- [ ] Publish the next field note.

> [!NOTE]
> This page intentionally includes table, task list, and alert Markdown so the portfolio prose styles can be verified.

pages.title: Contact
pages.excerpt: Get in touch with Jun Park about a project or collaboration.

pages.title: Northwind Banking
pages.content:
## Overview

Northwind is a regional bank with deep roots and an aging digital experience. We rebuilt the core banking app from the ground up — focusing on calm, glanceable information and a design system that lets a small team ship consistently across web, iOS, and Android.

## Responsibilities

- Defined product strategy with the executive team
- Built the foundational design system and component library
- Led research and usability testing across 4 markets
- Partnered with engineering on the SwiftUI and React rewrite
pages.excerpt: Redesigning a 30-year-old banking platform for a new generation of customers.

pages.title: Field Notes CMS
pages.content:
## Overview

Built for foreign correspondents who file from cafés, trains, and conflict zones, Field Notes is an offline-first writing tool with a quiet editing surface and a powerful sync engine.

## Responsibilities

- Designed the writing and review experience
- Implemented the offline-first sync layer
- Shipped the first public release in 6 months
pages.excerpt: A minimal headless CMS for journalists working in the field with intermittent connectivity.

pages.title: Atlas Type Foundry
pages.content:
## Overview

A complete identity system and commerce experience for a small foundry releasing two retail families a year. The site doubles as a specimen — every page is set in Atlas's own typefaces.

## Responsibilities

- Designed the wordmark and identity system
- Built the specimen-driven storefront
- Set up licensing and checkout flow
pages.excerpt: Identity and storefront for an independent type foundry based in Seoul.

pages.title: Marlow Coffee
pages.content:
## Overview

A direct-to-consumer storefront with a subscription engine, paired with a packaging refresh that travels well from shelf to shipping box.

## Responsibilities

- Designed the storefront and subscription flow
- Refreshed bag and box packaging
- Wrote product copy with the founders
pages.excerpt: An e-commerce experience for a single-origin coffee roaster in Portland.

pages.title: Harbor Health
pages.content:
## Overview

Harbor serves 14 community clinics across the Pacific Northwest. We replaced a fragmented portal with a single calm interface for booking, records, and messaging — built with accessibility as the baseline, not an afterthought.

## Responsibilities

- Led discovery interviews with patients and clinicians
- Designed the booking, records, and messaging surfaces
- Documented WCAG 2.2 AA patterns for the team
pages.excerpt: A patient-first booking and records app for a network of community clinics.

pages.title: Meridian OS
pages.content:
## Overview

Meridian needed one visual language across a CLI dashboard, a desktop client, and a marketing site. We shipped a token-driven system with theming, density modes, and a documentation site that engineers actually read.

## Responsibilities

- Architected design tokens and theming primitives
- Built 60+ React components with full a11y coverage
- Wrote and maintained the documentation site
pages.excerpt: A cross-platform design system powering a suite of developer tools.

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.content:
This Privacy Policy describes how junpark.studio ("we", "our") collects, uses, and shares information about you when you visit this website. This is a sample document for a portfolio site and should not be relied upon as legal advice.

## 1. Information we collect

We collect a minimal amount of information necessary to operate the site. This includes anonymous analytics data such as the pages you visit, your approximate location at the country level, and the device and browser you use.

## 2. How we use your information

- To understand which pages are most useful to visitors.
- To detect, prevent, and respond to abuse or technical issues.
- To respond to messages you send us through the contact page.

## 3. Sharing

We do not sell your personal information. We share data only with service providers who help us run the site, and only as needed to perform their work for us.

## 4. Your rights

Depending on where you live, you may have the right to access, correct, or delete the personal information we hold about you. To exercise these rights, email privacy@junpark.studio.

## 5. Contact

If you have any questions about this policy, please write to privacy@junpark.studio.

pages.title: Terms of Service
pages.content:
These Terms of Service govern your use of junpark.studio. By accessing the site you agree to these terms. This is a sample document provided as part of a portfolio template.

## 1. Use of the site

You may browse and read the content on this site for personal, non-commercial use. You may not scrape, republish, or use the content to train machine-learning models without prior written consent.

## 2. Intellectual property

All written content, project case studies, photographs, and design work are © Jun Park unless otherwise noted. Client work is shown with permission and remains the property of the respective clients.

## 3. Disclaimer

The site is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We make no guarantees about the accuracy or completeness of the information published here.

## 4. Limitation of liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, we are not liable for any indirect or consequential damages arising from your use of the site.

## 5. Changes

We may update these terms from time to time. The date at the top of the page indicates when the terms were last revised.

## 6. Governing law

These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Korea.
pages.excerpt: The terms that govern use of this site.

pages.title: Cookie Policy
pages.content:
This Cookie Policy explains how junpark.studio uses cookies and similar technologies when you visit the site. This is a sample document for a portfolio template.

## 1. What are cookies

Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They are widely used to make sites work, or work more efficiently, and to provide information to the site owner.

## 2. Cookies we use

- **Essential** — required for the site to function, such as remembering your light or dark mode preference.
- **Analytics** — anonymous, aggregated statistics about how visitors use the site. No personal identifiers are stored.

## 3. Managing cookies

Most browsers let you refuse or delete cookies. Doing so may affect parts of the site, such as your saved theme preference.

## 4. Third parties

We do not use third-party advertising cookies. Any analytics provider we use is contractually required to handle data on our behalf only.

## 5. Contact

Questions about cookies can be sent to privacy@junpark.studio.
pages.excerpt: How we use cookies on this site.
